Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (criminal interest rate)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (criminal interest rate)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Criminal Code to lower the threshold at which an interest rate becomes a criminal rate and to include, in the calculation of the interest rate, the charges paid by a person to obtain insurance coverage.
It also repeals section 347.1 of that Act respecting payday loan agreements.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: The definitions criminal rate and interest in subsection 347(2) of the Criminal Code are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: criminal rateâmeans an effective annual rate of interest on the credit advanced under an agreement or arrangement, calculated in accordance with generally accepted actuarial practices and principles, that, on the day on which the agreement or arrangement is entered into or renewed, exceeds by thirty percentage points or more the Bank of Canadaâs overnight rate on that day; (taux criminel)

interestâmeans the aggregate of all charges and expenses, whether in the form of a fee, fine, penalty, commission, insurance charge or other similar charge or expense or in any other form, paid or payable for the advancing of credit under an agreement or arrangement, by or on behalf of the person to whom the credit is or is to be advanced, irrespective of the person to whom any such charges and expenses are or are to be paid or payable, but does not include any repayment of credit advanced or any official fee, overdraft charge, required deposit balance or, in the case of a mortgage transaction, any amount required to be paid on account of property taxes; (intÃ©rÃªt)

Section.Text: Section 347.1 of the Act is repealed.
